deck.notes: Primary artifact for this eval is a harmonized counts export rather than a deck.

session.notes: Browser session export with workspace and harmonization session state.

scores.notes: Two ELSA tables remained materialized in one worker session and the bounded harmonized table was built without error.
The successful path was browser-native; the checked-in MCP workflow remains explicitly single-dataset.
Discovery was viable on this adjacent-file slice, but the eval did not deeply stress semantic narrowing.
Browser workspace harmonization is real and exportable, though the reproducible harness still leans on in-page store actions.
The run produced a reviewable harmonized counts export and a portable session, but not a more polished first-class harmonized export.
Auto-match proposed hundreds of candidates and the selected construct matched exactly with score 1.0 and no warnings.
Tight scope control kept the run inspectable: one pair, one construct, one confirmed mapping.

notableFindings: Adjacent ELSA Wave 4 and Wave 5 IFS-derived files can coexist in one browser workspace session with separate materialized tables.
The exact self-reported-health construct `srh3_hrs` auto-matched with score 1.0 and identical value labels across both waves.
Valid health-response distributions were effectively unchanged across the two waves: 41.9/31.9/26.2 vs 42.0/31.8/26.2.
followUps: Add an agent-facing multi-dataset / workspace path so harmonization is not browser-only in practice.
Preserve original wave numbers directly in harmonized outputs instead of generic source/target wave markers.
Use this bounded harmonization baseline to move execution to EVAL-06.
